perturbation.original_text: Whereas, Writer has written a script (“Submission”) for a possible future theatrical or motion picture production. Whereas, Writer wishes Producer to evaluate said “Submission” for the sole purpose of determining whether said Submission may be further developed into a motion picture (“Project”).
perturbation.changed_text: Whereas, Writer has written a script (“Work Product”) for a possible future theatrical or motion picture production. Whereas, Writer wishes Producer to evaluate said “Submission” for the sole purpose of determining whether said Submission may be further developed into a motion picture (“Project”).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Submission' is replaced with 'Work Product' in the first sentence of the 'Whereas' clause, but it remains 'Submission' in the second sentence of the 'Whereas' clause. This introduces uncertainty as to whether the terms 'Submission' and 'Work Product' are interchangeable or if they refer to different scripts entirely. This directly contradicts the subsequent sections referencing 'Submission' as the key material under consideration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. 1. All information disclosed by Writer to Producer, in writing, whether or not such information is also disclosed orally, th at relates or refers, directly or i ndirectly, to the Submission, including the Submission itself, shall be deemed confidential an d shall constitute Confidential Information, and shall include (i) all documents generated by Producer which contain, comment upon, or relate in any way to any Confidential Information received form Writer, and ( ii) any written samples of the Submission received from Writer together with any information derived by Producer there from.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Submission' with 'Work Product' in the first sentence while retaining 'Submission' in the second sentence of the 'Whereas' clause. The explanation states this introduces uncertainty about whether 'Submission' and 'Work Product' are interchangeable, contradicting sections referencing 'Submission' as the key material. The 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'Submission' and information related to it as confidential, creating a contradiction because the changed text now uses 'Work Product' and 'Submission' in the same clause, creating uncertainty regarding which script the contract refers to. Since Misaligned Terminology leads to **in-text contradictions** when the contract **uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them**, leading to conflicting obligations as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, it is a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: .2. Confidential Information shall not include any information: .(i) That Producer can show by documentary evidence was known to Producer or prior to the date of its disclosure to Producer by Writer or .(ii) That becomes publicly known, by publication or otherwise, not due to any unauthorized act or omission of Producer or any other party having an obligation of confidentiality to Writer; or, .(iii) That is subsequently disclos3ed by Writer to any person, firm or corporation on a on-confidential basis; or .(iv) That producer can conclusively show by documentary evidence that such information was developed independent of any access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: .2. Confidential Information shall not include any intelligence: .(i) That Producer can show by documentary evidence was known to Producer or prior to the date of its disclosure to Producer by Writer or .(ii) That becomes publicly known, by publication or otherwise, not due to any unauthorized act or omission of Producer or any other party having an obligation of confidentiality to Writer; or, .(iii) That is subsequently disclos3ed by Writer to any person, firm or corporation on a on-confidential basis; or .(iv) That producer can conclusively show by documentary evidence that such information was developed independent of any access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'intelligence' creates ambiguity. The contract defines 'Confidential Information' earlier. Using 'intelligence' here suggests it's a different type of information, which creates an in-text contradiction because the contract relies on the original definition of 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. 5. The Confidential Information shall rema in the property of Writer and shall not be disclosed or revealed by Producer or anyone else except employees of Producer who have a need to know the information in connection with Producer’s evalua tion of the Submission, and who have entered into a secrecy agreement with Producer under which such employees are required to keep confidential the Confidential Information of Writer, and such employees shall be advised by Producer of the confidential nature of the information and that the information shall be treated accordingly. Producer shall be liable for any improper disclosure of the Confidential Information by its employees.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information', and the contradicted text relies on this definition when specifying that the 'Confidential Information' remains the property of the Writer and dictates how Producer should handle and protect it. By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'intelligence', the changed text introduces ambiguity regarding which information is subject to the confidentiality obligations outlined in the contradicted section. This creates a direct conflict, as it's unclear whether the confidentiality provisions now apply to 'intelligence' or only to the originally defined 'Confidential Information', making the obligations less clear and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, indicating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Writer will disclose the Confidential Information to Producer solely for the purpose of allowing Producer to evaluate the Submission to dete rmine, in its sole discretion, whether the Submission may be further developed into a Project.
perturbation.changed_text: Writer will disclose the Proprietary Material to Producer solely for the purpose of allowing Producer to evaluate the Submission to dete rmine, in its sole discretion, whether the Submission may be further developed into a Project.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Proprietary Material'. Because 'Proprietary Material' is not defined, it's unclear if it's synonymous with 'Confidential Information' or something else entirely. This creates confusion about what exactly Writer is disclosing and what obligations Producer has, directly contradicting the intent of Clause 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. 1. All information disclosed by Writer to Producer, in writing, whether or not such information is also disclosed orally, th at relates or refers, directly or i ndirectly, to the Submission, including the Submission itself, shall be deemed confidential an d shall constitute Confidential Information, and shall include (i) all documents generated by Producer which contain, comment upon, or relate in any way to any Confidential Information received form Writer, and ( ii) any written samples of the Submission received from Writer together with any information derived by Producer there from.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which Clause 1.1 defines explicitly. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Material', which is undefined in the contract. This creates a contradiction because the contract specifies that 'Confidential Information' is defined in Clause 1.1, while 'Proprietary Material' has no such definition, rendering the obligations of the producer unclear and potentially unenforceable. This is a clear misalignment of terminology as per the definition.

perturbation.original_text: Other than as specifically provided herein, Producer will not use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever other than for the sole purpose permitted in paragraph 3 hereof, unless and until a further executed agreement is first made between the parties setting forth the terms and conditions under which rights to the Submission and the Confidential Information are to be licensed to, or acquired by, Producer.
perturbation.changed_text: Other than as specifically provided herein, Producer will not use the Trade Secret for any purpose whatsoever other than for the sole purpose permitted in paragraph 3 hereof, unless and until a further executed agreement is first made between the parties setting forth the terms and conditions under which rights to the Submission and the Confidential Information are to be licensed to, or acquired by, Producer.
perturbation.explanation: The substitution of 'Confidential Information' with 'Trade Secret' introduces a significant ambiguity. 'Trade Secret' has a specific legal meaning, implying a higher level of protection and specific requirements (e.g., economic value derived from secrecy). This clashes with the existing definition and scope of 'Confidential Information' laid out earlier in the contract. Also, the term 'Confidential Information' still exists in the same sentence, creating a clear case of in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: .10. Producer’s obligations under paragraphs 3,4, and 8 of this agreement shall extend from the date of this agreement and shall survive the expiration or term ination of this agreement, provided, however, that Producer’s obligations under paragraphs 3 and 4 of this agreement shall terminate immediately in the event that Writer .shall purposefully disclose the Confidential Information to any other person, firm, or corporation on a non-confidential basis, during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Trade Secret' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Confidential Information' and outlines specific conditions for its protection and termination of obligations related to it. The change introduces ambiguity and potential conflict because 'Trade Secret' has a different legal meaning than 'Confidential Information', as the explanation states. Since both terms ('Trade Secret' and 'Confidential Information') appear in the Changed Text, there is a direct in-text contradiction with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Writer hereby expressly warrants that it has the full right and authority to disclose the Confidential Information to Producer, and that no prior public non-confidential disclosure of the Confidential Information ahs been made by Writer nor, to the best of Writer’s knowledge, by any other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Writer hereby expressly warrants that it has the full right and authority to disclose the Protected Data to Producer, and that no prior public non-confidential disclosure of the Confidential Information ahs been made by Writer nor, to the best of Writer’s knowledge, by any other party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data', while keeping Confidential Information on the second part of the sentence, introduces a contradiction because 'Protected Data' is undefined in this contract, directly contradicting with the confidential information scope that's supposed to be reffered in that section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. 12. Nothing in this agreement shall be deemed a sale or offer for sale of the Submission, and nothing contained herein shall in any way obligate Writer to grant to Producer a license or any other rights, directly or by implication, estoppel or otherwise to the Confidential Information or the Submission.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data', but the second part of the sentence and the 'Contradicted Text' still refer to 'Confidential Information'. Because 'Protected Data' is undefined, while the rest of the contract mentions 'Confidential Information', this creates a direct contradiction and ambiguity regarding what information the Writer is warranting and what obligations pertain to that data, as specified in the Misaligned Terminology definition.
